title: INSERT with timestamp in DST non-existent range results in '0000-00-00' instead as adjusted to next valid time, as in MySQL
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

During Daylight Saving Time, DST, the time '2025-03-30 02:00:00'~'2025-03-30 02:59:00' does not exist. When I insert such a invalid data into TiDB , the result is '0000-00-00' and different compared with MySQL 8.0.39, which adjusts it to the next valid time.

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
SET time_zone = 'Europe/Stockholm';
set sql_mode='';
CREATE TABLE `t1` (
   id int,
  `created` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
);
insert into t1 values(1,'2025-03-30 01:59:00');
insert into t1 values(2,'2025-03-30 02:00:00');
insert into t1 values(3,'2025-03-30 02:59:00');
insert into t1 values(4,'2025-03-30 03:00:00');
select * from t1;
```



### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql> select * from t1;
+------+---------------------+
| id   | created             |
+------+---------------------+
|    1 | 2025-03-30 01:59:00 |
|    2 | 2025-03-30 03:00:00 |
|    3 | 2025-03-30 03:00:00 |
|    4 | 2025-03-30 03:00:00 |
+------+---------------------+
4 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```
mysql> select * from t1;
+------+---------------------+
| id   | created             |
+------+---------------------+
|    1 | 2025-03-30 01:59:00 |
|    2 | 0000-00-00 00:00:00 |
|    3 | 0000-00-00 00:00:00 |
|    4 | 2025-03-30 03:00:00 |
+------+---------------------+
4 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

v8.5.1
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO t2 (event_time, description) VALUES ('2025-03-30 01:59:00', 'Valid timestamp before DST gap');
INSERT INTO t2 (event_time, description) VALUES (NULLIF('2025-03-30 02:00:00', '2025-03-30 02:00:00'), 'Invalid timestamp during DST gap');
INSERT INTO t2 (event_time, description) VALUES ('2025-03-30 03:00:00', 'Valid timestamp after DST gap');
key_bug_features: INSERT with timestamp in non-existent DST range
